hints.title: What changes between sessions?
hints.content: Notice the duplicate messages get worse the more times a user reconnects. Look at what gets created on each connect - and what (if anything) gets cleaned up on each disconnect.

hints.title: Lifecycle mismatch
hints.content: Compare onConnection() and onDisconnect(). Is there cleanup symmetry? A subscriber is allocated in one but never released in the other.

hints.title: Track and clean
hints.content: You need to track each socket's subscriber so you can find and stop it on disconnect. A Map<socketId, SubscriberClient> stored on the ChatServer instance is the natural fit.

hints.title: Solution outline
hints.content:
1) Add `private subs = new Map<string, SubscriberClient>()` to ChatServer.
2) In onConnection: after creating the subscriber, do `this.subs.set(socketId, sub)`.
3) In onDisconnect: look up the sub by socketId, call `sub.unsubscribe()` and `sub.quit()`, then `this.subs.delete(socketId)`.
